items.title: ActionParty: Multi-Subject Action Binding in Generative Video Games
items.authors: Alexander Pondaven, Ziyi Wu, Igor Gilitschenski et al.
items.abstract: Recent advances in video diffusion have enabled the development of "world models" capable of simulating interactive environments. However, these models are largely restricted to single-agent settings, failing to control multiple agents simultaneously in a scene. In this work, we tackle a fundamental...

items.title: Steerable Visual Representations
items.authors: Jona Ruthardt, Manu Gaur, Deva Ramanan et al.
items.abstract: Pretrained Vision Transformers (ViTs) such as DINOv2 and MAE provide generic image features that can be applied to a variety of downstream tasks such as retrieval, classification, and segmentation. However, such representations tend to focus on the most salient visual cues in the image, with no way ...

items.title: Grounded Token Initialization for New Vocabulary in LMs for Generative Recommendation
items.authors: Daiwei Chen, Zhoutong Fu, Chengming Jiang et al.
items.abstract: Language models (LMs) are increasingly extended with new learnable vocabulary tokens for domain-specific tasks, such as Semantic-ID tokens in generative recommendation. The standard practice initializes these new tokens as the mean of existing vocabulary embeddings, then relies on supervised fine-tu...

items.title: Batched Contextual Reinforcement: A Task-Scaling Law for Efficient Reasoning
items.authors: Bangji Yang, Hongbo Ma, Jiajun Fan et al.
items.abstract: Large Language Models employing Chain-of-Thought reasoning achieve strong performance but suffer from excessive token consumption that inflates inference costs. Existing efficiency methods such as explicit length penalties, difficulty estimators, or multi-stage curricula either degrade reasoning qua...

items.title: EventHub: Data Factory for Generalizable Event-Based Stereo Networks without Active Sensors
items.authors: Luca Bartolomei, Fabio Tosi, Matteo Poggi et al.
items.abstract: We propose EventHub, a novel framework for training deep-event stereo networks without ground truth annotations from costly active sensors, relying instead on standard color images. From these images, we derive either proxy annotations and proxy events through state-of-the-art novel view synthesis t...
